title: Grit: The Power of Passion and Perseverance | Angela Lee Duckworth | TED

transcript.chinese: 抄写员：Joseph Geni 审稿人：Morton Bast
transcript.english: Transcriber: Joseph Geni Reviewer: Morton Bast

transcript.chinese: 当我 27 岁时，我辞去了一份要求非常高的管理咨询工作，转而从事了一份要求更高的工作：教学。
transcript.english: When I was 27 years old, I left a very demanding job in management consulting for a job that was even more demanding: teaching.

transcript.chinese: 我去纽约市公立学校教七年级学生数学。
transcript.english: I went to teach seventh graders math in the New York City public schools.

transcript.chinese: 像任何老师一样，我做了测验和测试。
transcript.english: And like any teacher, I made quizzes and tests.

transcript.chinese: 我布置了家庭作业。
transcript.english: I gave out homework assignments.

transcript.chinese: 作业回来后，我计算了成绩。
transcript.english: When the work came back, I calculated grades.

transcript.chinese: 让我震惊的是，智商并不是我最好的学生和最差的学生之间的唯一区别。
transcript.english: What struck me was that IQ was not the only difference between my best and my worst students.

transcript.chinese: 我的一些表现最好的人的智商分数并不高。
transcript.english: Some of my strongest performers did not have stratospheric IQ scores.

transcript.chinese: 我的一些最聪明的孩子表现不佳。
transcript.english: Some of my smartest kids weren't doing so well.

transcript.chinese: 这让我开始思考。
transcript.english: And that got me thinking.

transcript.chinese: 当然，你在七年级数学中需要学习的东西是很难的：比率、小数、平行四边形的面积。
transcript.english: The kinds of things you need to learn in seventh grade math, sure, they're hard: ratios, decimals, the area of a parallelogram.

transcript.chinese: 但这些概念并非不可能，我坚信，如果我的每一位学生足够努力和足够长时间，他们都可以学习这些材料。
transcript.english: But these concepts are not impossible, and I was firmly convinced that every one of my students could learn the material if they worked hard and long enough.

transcript.chinese: 经过几年的教学，我得出的结论是，我们在教育中需要的是从动机的角度、从心理学的角度更好地理解学生和学习。
transcript.english: After several more years of teaching, I came to the conclusion that what we need in education is a much better understanding of students and learning from a motivational perspective, from a psychological perspective.

transcript.chinese: 在教育领域，我们知道如何最好地衡量的一件事就是智商。
transcript.english: In education, the one thing we know how to measure best is IQ.

transcript.chinese: 但是，如果在学校和生活中取得好成绩不仅仅取决于您快速轻松学习的能力，该怎么办？
transcript.english: But what if doing well in school and in life depends on much more than your ability to learn quickly and easily?

transcript.chinese: 所以我离开了教室，去读研究生，成为一名心理学家。
transcript.english: So I left the classroom, and I went to graduate school to become a psychologist.

transcript.chinese: 我开始在各种超级具有挑战性的环境中研究儿童和成人，在每项研究中我的问题是，谁在这里取得了成功，为什么？
transcript.english: I started studying kids and adults in all kinds of super challenging settings, and in every study my question was, who is successful here and why?

transcript.chinese: 我和我的研究团队去了西点军校。
transcript.english: My research team and I went to West Point Military Academy.

transcript.chinese: 我们试图预测哪些学员会继续接受军事训练，哪些会退出。
transcript.english: We tried to predict which cadets would stay in military training and which would drop out.

transcript.chinese: 我们参加了全国拼字比赛，试图预测哪些孩子会在比赛中取得最大进步。
transcript.english: We went to the National Spelling Bee and tried to predict which children would advance farthest in competition.

transcript.chinese: 我们研究了在非常艰苦的社区工作的新教师，询问哪些教师在学年结束时仍然会在这里教学，而在这些教师中，谁将最有效地提高学生的学习成果？
transcript.english: We studied rookie teachers working in really tough neighborhoods, asking which teachers are still going to be here in teaching by the end of the school year, and of those, who will be the most effective at improving learning outcomes for their students?

transcript.chinese: 我们与私营公司合作，询问这些销售人员中哪些人会保住工作？
transcript.english: We partnered with private companies, asking, which of these salespeople is going to keep their jobs?

transcript.chinese: 谁会赚最多的钱？
transcript.english: And who's going to earn the most money?

transcript.chinese: 在所有这些截然不同的背景下，有一个特征成为成功的重要预测因素。
transcript.english: In all those very different contexts, one characteristic emerged as a significant predictor of success.

transcript.chinese: 这不是社交智力。
transcript.english: And it wasn't social intelligence.

transcript.chinese: 不是漂亮的外表，不是身体健康，也不是智商。
transcript.english: It wasn't good looks, physical health, and it wasn't IQ.

transcript.chinese: 这是砂砾。
transcript.english: It was grit.

transcript.chinese: 毅力是对长期目标的热情和毅力。
transcript.english: Grit is passion and perseverance for very long-term goals.

transcript.chinese: 毅力就是有耐力。
transcript.english: Grit is having stamina.

transcript.chinese: 毅力就是日复一日地坚持你的未来，不仅仅是一周，不仅仅是一个月，而是几年，并非常努力地使未来成为现实。
transcript.english: Grit is sticking with your future, day in, day out, not just for the week, not just for the month, but for years, and working really hard to make that future a reality.

transcript.chinese: 坚毅就是让生​​活像一场马拉松，而不是短跑。
transcript.english: Grit is living life like it's a marathon, not a sprint.

transcript.chinese: 几年前，我开始在芝加哥公立学校学习毅力。
transcript.english: A few years ago, I started studying grit in the Chicago public schools.

transcript.chinese: 我要求数千名高中生接受毅力调查问卷，然后等待了大约一年多的时间，看看谁会毕业。
transcript.english: I asked thousands of high school juniors to take grit questionnaires, and then waited around more than a year to see who would graduate.

transcript.chinese: 事实证明，更坚强的孩子毕业的可能性要大得多，即使我在我能衡量的每一个特征上都与他们相匹配，比如家庭收入、标准化成绩测试分数，甚至孩子们在学校时的安全感。
transcript.english: Turns out that grittier kids were significantly more likely to graduate, even when I matched them on every characteristic I could measure, things like family income, standardized achievement test scores, even how safe kids felt when they were at school.

transcript.chinese: 因此，毅力不仅在西点军校或全国拼字比赛中很重要。
transcript.english: So it's not just at West Point or the National Spelling Bee that grit matters.

transcript.chinese: 在学校里也是如此，尤其是对于有辍学风险的孩子。
transcript.english: It's also in school, especially for kids at risk for dropping out.

transcript.chinese: 对我来说，关于毅力最令人震惊的事情是我们对毅力的培养知之甚少，科学对它的了解也是如此之少。
transcript.english: To me, the most shocking thing about grit is how little we know, how little science knows, about building it.

transcript.chinese: 每天，家长和老师都会问我：“我如何培养孩子们的毅力？
transcript.english: Every day, parents and teachers ask me, "How do I build grit in kids?

transcript.chinese: 我该如何教导孩子们扎实的职业道德？
transcript.english: What do I do to teach kids a solid work ethic?

transcript.chinese: 如何让他们长期保持积极性？”诚实的回答是，我不知道。
transcript.english: How do I keep them motivated for the long run?" The honest answer is, I don't know.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）我所知道的是，才华并不会让你变得坚韧。
transcript.english: (Laughter) What I do know is that talent doesn't make you gritty.

transcript.chinese: 我们的数据非常清楚地表明，有许多有才华的人根本没有兑现自己的承诺。
transcript.english: Our data show very clearly that there are many talented individuals who simply do not follow through on their commitments.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，在我们的数据中，毅力通常与人才衡量标准无关，甚至成反比。
transcript.english: In fact, in our data, grit is usually unrelated or even inversely related to measures of talent.

transcript.chinese: 到目前为止，我听过的关于培养孩子毅力的最好想法就是所谓的“成长心态”。这是斯坦福大学卡罗尔·德韦克（Carol Dweck）提出的一个想法，它相信学习能力不是固定的，它可以随着你的努力而改变。
transcript.english: So far, the best idea I've heard about building grit in kids is something called "growth mindset." This is an idea developed at Stanford University by Carol Dweck, and it is the belief that the ability to learn is not fixed, that it can change with your effort.

transcript.chinese: 博士。
transcript.english: Dr.

transcript.chinese: 德韦克表明，当孩子们阅读和了解大脑以及大脑如何因应对挑战而变化和成长时，他们在失败时更有可能坚持下去，因为他们不相信失败是一种永久性的情况。
transcript.english: Dweck has shown that when kids read and learn about the brain and how it changes and grows in response to challenge, they're much more likely to persevere when they fail, because they don't believe that failure is a permanent condition.

transcript.chinese: 因此，成长心态是培养毅力的好主意。
transcript.english: So growth mindset is a great idea for building grit.

transcript.chinese: 但我们还需要更多。
transcript.english: But we need more.

transcript.chinese: 我的发言就到此结束，因为这就是我们现在的处境。
transcript.english: And that's where I'm going to end my remarks, because that's where we are.

transcript.chinese: 这就是摆在我们面前的工作。
transcript.english: That's the work that stands before us.

transcript.chinese: 我们需要采取最好的想法、最强烈的直觉，并且需要测试它们。
transcript.english: We need to take our best ideas, our strongest intuitions, and we need to test them.

transcript.chinese: 我们需要衡量我们是否成功，并且我们必须愿意失败，愿意犯错，并从经验教训中重新开始。
transcript.english: We need to measure whether we've been successful, and we have to be willing to fail, to be wrong, to start over again with lessons learned.

transcript.chinese: 换句话说，我们需要坚韧不拔，让我们的孩子变得更加坚韧。
transcript.english: In other words, we need to be gritty about getting our kids grittier.

transcript.chinese: 谢谢。
transcript.english: Thank you.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）
transcript.english: (Applause)
